assumptions: Match engine: independent Poisson goals; each team's expected goals set by the Elo-rating gap (host nations +50 Elo).
Top teams' strength is fitted to the betting market's title odds (Kalshi via Covers, 13 Jun 2026); the rest of the field is set by world-football Elo (15 Jun 2026).

Second calibration input: any scheduled but unplayed group game that has a betting line in match_odds.json is priced directly off the market's 1X2 (home/draw/away) odds — the model solves the Poisson scoring rates that reproduce that line — instead of inferring the game from the Elo gap.
Completed results so far are locked in; all remaining group games and every knockout tie are simulated.
Knockout ties level after 90' are resolved by an Elo-logistic extra-time/penalty model.
Best-8 third-placed teams allocated to bracket slots using FIFA's exact Annex C table (495 combinations).
Probabilities from 40,000 Monte Carlo tournament simulations; sampling error on a 50% probability is about ±0.3%.
A few lower-tier teams' Elo values are estimated (flagged in data); they barely affect the knockout picture and refine as results post.

matchups.why: Egypt replaces Belgium as Czechia's projected opponent — driven by Belgium 0-0 Iran (Group G) and a re-priced New Zealand–Egypt betting line. Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 7.8%) — a lean, not a lock.

Iran replaces Algeria as Canada's projected opponent — driven by Belgium 0-0 Iran (Group G) and a re-priced Jordan–Algeria betting line. Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 14%) — a lean, not a lock.
Belgium replaces Egypt as Australia's projected opponent — driven by Belgium 0-0 Iran (Group G) and a re-priced New Zealand–Egypt betting line.
Norway replaces Côte d'Ivoire as Brazil's projected opponent — driven by a re-priced Norway–Senegal betting line. Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 13%) — a lean, not a lock.
Croatia replaces Portugal as Spain's projected opponent — driven by a re-priced Portugal–Uzbekistan betting line.
Portugal replaces Colombia as Canada's projected opponent — driven by a re-priced Portugal–Uzbekistan betting line. Still a wide-open slot (top pairing just 11%) — a lean, not a lock.

changes.summary: This rebuild ingested 2 new results (Spain 4-0 Saudi Arabia (Group H); Belgium 0-0 Iran (Group G)) and 14 re-priced game lines. 6 bracket boxes now show a different pairing — see each for what moved it.
